headline: Quantum Frontiers: From Network Fiber to Entanglement Math
summary: Today's research spans the entire quantum stack, from physical hardware defects and metropolitan-scale networks to foundational mathematical proofs of entropy and circuit optimization. Together, these papers advance our ability to scale, measure, and theoretically model quantum information systems.

themes.title: Quantum Networks and Hardware Realities
themes.body: Researchers are tackling the physical challenges of quantum scaling. This includes demonstrating a stable, continuous 150-hour six-user metropolitan quantum network and identifying Coulomb blockade in microscopic metallic grains as a major source of circuit decoherence.

themes.title: Entanglement Dynamics and Detection
themes.body: New theoretical and practical frameworks are improving how we understand and measure entanglement. Work includes bounding deep thermalisation timescales via entanglement teleportation and bypassing state tomography to measure 2-qubit and 3-qubit entanglement directly.

themes.title: Optimization and Information Theory
themes.body: Mathematical foundations of quantum computing see major updates. A new backpropagation algorithm slashes the memory cost of optimizing quantum circuits, while a separate proof narrows the unresolved interval for the additivity of minimum output Rényi entropy.

connections.body: While paper 2607.15201 designs efficient circuits to directly measure entanglement, paper 2607.15184 introduces a backpropagation algorithm to optimize quantum circuits with minimal memory overhead.
